dev.jeka.core.api.system

Class JkLog

  • java.lang.Object
    • dev.jeka.core.api.system.JkLog
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    java.io.Serializable

    public final class JkLog
extends java.lang.Object
implements java.io.Serializable
    Provides static methods for logging events.

    Events are simply forwarded to an event consumer which has the responsibility to handle it. A basic handler may simply display events on the console. That is the case for the Jeka tool. By default, logging events turns in a no-op.

    This class provides task concept for hierarchical logs. A log event happening during a task will be assigned a nested task level (task can be nested). This feature mekes hierarchical logging possible.

    See Also:
    Serialized Form

    • Constructor Detail

      • JkLog

        public JkLog()

    • Method Detail

      • setDecorator

        public static void setDecorator(JkLog.JkLogDecorator newDecorator)
        By default, events are not consumed, meaning nothing appends when info(String, Object...) (String), error(String, Object...) (String)}, warn(String, Object...) (String)} or verbose(String, Object...) (String)} are invoked. Therefore, users have to set explicitly a consumer using this method or setDecorator(Style) ().

      • setDecorator

        public static void setDecorator(JkLog.Style style)
        This set the default consumer. This consumer displays logs in the console in a hierarchical style.

      • setLogOnlyOnStdErr

        public static void setLogOnlyOnStdErr(boolean flag)
        if true, all log will be printed to stderr instead og stdout.
        Parameters:
        flag -

      • restoreToInitialState

        public static void restoreToInitialState()
        Let JkLog returns to its initial state, meaning events are not output and System.out/err are back to the state found when this class was loaded.

      • redirect

        public static void redirect(java.io.PrintStream out,
                            java.io.PrintStream err)

        public static void setVerbosity(JkLog.Verbosity verbosityArg)

      • startTask

        public static void startTask(java.lang.String message,
                             java.lang.Object... params)
        Logs the start of the current task. Subsequent logs will be nested in this task log until #endTask is invoked.

      • verboseStartTask

        public static void verboseStartTask(java.lang.String message,
                                    java.lang.Object... params)
        Logs the start of the current task. !!! Should be closed by verboseEndTask().

      • debugStartTask

        public static void debugStartTask(java.lang.String message,
                                  java.lang.Object... params)
        Logs the start of the current task. !!! Should be closed by verboseEndTask().

      • endTask

        public static void endTask(java.lang.String message)
        Logs the end of the current task with a specific message. The specified message is formatted by String#format passing the duration time in milliseconds as argument. So if your message contains '%d', it will be replaced by the duration taken to complete the current task.

      • endTask

        public static void endTask()
        Same as endTask(String) but using the standard message.

        public static void verboseEndTask()

        public static void debugEndTask()
